summary: Iteration-3 (Supabase + Groq migration) verified end-to-end. Backend: 21/21 pytest passed (16 existing test_belief_ledger.py + 5 new test_supabase_migration.py covering JSONB roundtrip, cascade delete, edge-kind validation, Postgres persistence, multi-session seed). Total backend runtime ~110s. Frontend: dashboard loads, 4 tabs present (tab-ledger, tab-graph, tab-crux, tab-scanner) with always-shown WRITE_ENTRY left panel. [demo] button now seeds 7 beliefs + 8 revisions (HUGE improvement over iteration_2 which only seeded 1 — Groq + json_object response_format clearly fixed the silent-fallback issue). /api/graph returns 7 nodes / 3 links with both 'contradicts' and 'supports' kinds. /api/cruxes returns 3 items each with 3 cruxes. Supabase transaction pooler (port 6543, statement_cache_size=0) is stable across all calls; no connection / 'prepared statement already exists' errors observed.

minor.issue: Still returns 200 with {count:0,results:[]} if LLM extraction yields zero beliefs (rare with Groq + json_object response_format). Carried over from iteration_2 — not a regression. Consider surfacing a parse_warning so UI can retry.

critical_code_review_comments: llm_service.py docstring still says 'wrapping Nebius Token Factory' but provider is now Groq — minor doc drift.
llm_service._chat comment ('Nebius (OpenAI-compatible) supports JSON-only response_format') is stale; Groq does support response_format=json_object so behaviour is correct, just rename the comment.
database.py: pool_pre_ping=False is intentional for transaction pooler but worth a comment so future engineers don't 'fix' it.

server.py seed_demo iterates DEMO_ENTRIES sequentially with a fresh AsyncSessionLocal per entry — correct after the migration; could be parallelised with asyncio.gather for ~4x speedup on cold demo seeds (currently ~30-45s total).

Carryover from prior iterations (still applicable): server.py is 514 lines and approaching the 700-line split threshold; ExtractionResult.runCrux still has no .catch handler (frontend); /api/cruxes still does blocking LLM calls inside an HTTP request.
Pydantic Belief response model is implicit (dict via belief_to_dict). Adding a Pydantic response_model would enforce the API contract and catch schema drift like the 'source_entry_id' case.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — header counter, [demo] seeding, all 4 tabs visible/clickable; /api/graph + /api/cruxes return populated payloads

seed_data_creation: test_supabase_migration.py creates/deletes its own entries and ends by wiping the DB via /api/reset. Demo seed (7 beliefs / 8 revisions) was left in place by the final frontend smoke run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Migration to Supabase + Groq is solid. The frontend tab testids are: tab-ledger, tab-graph, tab-crux (NOT tab-cruxes), tab-scanner — note the iteration_2 report mentioned tab-cruxes which is incorrect. WRITE_ENTRY panel is always shown on the left (no tab-write). seed-demo now reliably produces 7 beliefs + 8 revisions with both 'contradicts' and 'supports' edges — the iteration_2 'silent JSON parse fallback' issue is effectively resolved by the Groq json_object response_format. If you ever need to retest cascade delete or JSONB, use /app/backend/tests/test_supabase_migration.py.
rca of the issue: n/a — no blocking issues. Iteration_2's 'seed-demo only 1/4 beliefs' issue is RESOLVED by the provider switch (Groq + response_format=json_object). Reproduction of the fix: POST /api/seed-demo now returns {entries:4, seeded_beliefs>=4 typically 7+}, and /api/graph shows 7 nodes with both contradicts+supports kinds.
